behavior_summary: This script implements a palace guardsman NPC with: a simple U8P_GUARD10::look/description handler, fast-area activation (used to respawn/relocate or activate the guard), a scheduling/init routine that can finalize respawn, a theft-response handler that alerts other guards when certain item types are stolen, and a short conversation flow with a small removable menu.
U8P_GUARD10::look: when the player 'looks' at the NPC it emits a short bark — 'dead guardsman' if the NPC is flagged dead, otherwise 'guardsman'.

U8P_GUARD10::enterFastArea: triggered by entering the NPC's fast-area; if the NPC is dead it clears dead/in-combat state and teleports the NPC to fixed coordinates (map 0x29, x=0x64, y=0x64) to effectively revive/relocate it; if not dead it invokes an internal activation method and suspends.

U8P_GUARD10::schedule: writes local U8P_GUARD10::schedule temporaries, calls engine U8P_GUARD10::schedule/apply hooks, suspends, and on a positive process_result() clears dead and invokes a finalization method — effectively a scheduled respawn/activation path.

U8P_GUARD10::AvatarStoleSomething(uword): called when the Avatar steals an item; it checks Item::getType on the stolen item and, for specific guarded item types and if 'guardsCalled' is not set, barks 'Stop! Thief!' and calls CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime to alert other guards.

U8P_GUARD10::use: a short conversation process that picks one of a couple of greetings at random, builds a menu of four choices (Who are you?, What is your duty?, Where am I?, goodbye), removes options as they are asked, gives canned replies, and uses the global flag 'devonInRule' to choose the 'Where am I?' reply.
notable_control_flow: Look handler branches on Npc::isDead(this) to select one of two short barks and then suspends.
U8P_GUARD10::enterFastArea branches on Npc::isDead(this): the dead branch clears Npc::dead and in-combat flags then teleports the NPC; the alive branch calls METHOD::func15E7 then suspends.

U8P_GUARD10::schedule sets several local U8P_GUARD10::schedule temporaries, calls an engine scheduling method, suspends, then checks (word)process_result() and on true clears Npc::dead and calls METHOD::func133F before suspending again.

U8P_GUARD10::AvatarStoleSomething tests Item::getType(local1) against a set of guarded type constants; if matched and guards haven't yet been called, it barks, suspends, and invokes CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime.

U8P_GUARD10::use runs a conversation loop: Ask -> SuspendAssign local1 (menu choice) -> strcmp-based branches -> MenuRemove removes asked options -> loop or EndConversation on 'goodbye' or specific branches that terminate early.
open_questions: Exact semantics and effects of METHOD::func15E7, METHOD::func143A and METHOD::func133F: they appear to be engine/internal hooks for activation/scheduling but their precise role is inferred.
Whether teleport coordinates in U8P_GUARD10::enterFastArea always represent a canonical respawn point or a relocation for a specific patrol route (inferred as respawn/relocate).
Whether 'guardsCalled' is a local/global campaign flag elsewhere set by CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime or by other scripts; here it is only read, not written.
Precise engine behavior of the U8P_GUARD10::schedule suspend/process_result() handshake: inferred as a wait-for-engine-apply step but details (timing/conditions) are unknown.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD10::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/description response for this guardsman NPC; choose a 'dead guardsman' or 'guardsman' bark depending on the NPC's dead state.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player examines/looks at the NPC (standard U8P_GUARD10::look/examine event).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD10::look
function_map.role: simple U8P_GUARD10::look/examine handler producing short bark based on Npc::isDead

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD10::enterFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area trigger handler that revives/relocates or activates the NPC on area entry

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD10::schedule
function_map.role: initial scheduling/respawn initializer that calls engine U8P_GUARD10::schedule hooks and may finalize activation on process_result()

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD10::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: monologue-style reaction to theft: checks item type and alerts guards for guarded items

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD10::use
function_map.role: dialogue process: random greeting, present/remove small menu of questions, supply canned answers (conditional on devonInRule for location answer)

external_calls.purpose: Clear the NPC's dead flag (used to revive/reset the NPC in the fast-area dead branch).
Clear the NPC's in-combat state during revive/reset in fast-area handling.
Move the NPC to fixed map/coordinates (map 0x29, x=0x64, y=0x64) as part of revive/relocate behavior.
An internal/engine method called when entering fast-area and NPC is not dead; likely performs activation or AI setup (inferred).
Apply/register U8P_GUARD10::schedule or pass U8P_GUARD10::schedule state to the engine (inferred from scheduling context).
Check suspend return value to decide whether to continue with respawn/finalization.
Clear NPC dead flag when scheduling indicates activation should proceed (respawn path).
Query the stolen item's type so the script can decide whether the theft should trigger a guard response.
Alert/summon other guards when a guarded item type is stolen.
Part of conversation setup/engine housekeeping (returns a pid/temp used during dialog setup; exact effect unclear).
Engine housekeeping during conversation setup/teardown (exact semantics unclear).
Choose a random greeting variant at conversation start.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Used to select the 'Where am I?' reply in U8P_GUARD10::use; if true reply is 'You are in the palace, of course!', otherwise a hostile/terminating response is used.

reads.name: guardsCalled
reads.likely_effect: Checked (negated) in U8P_GUARD10::AvatarStoleSomething to prevent repeated guard alerts; script only reads it here.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a palace guardsman NPC with: a simple U8P_GUARD10::look/description handler, fast-area activation (used to respawn/relocate or activate the guard), a scheduling/init routine that can finalize respawn, a theft-response handler that alerts other guards when certain item types are stolen, and a short conversation flow with a small removable menu.
